pages.text:
Overview
Azure AI Search (formerly Azure Cognitive Search) is a fully managed search-as-a-service
platform that indexes structured and unstructured content, applies AI enrichment via
skillsets, and exposes full-text, vector, and hybrid query capabilities. It is the retrieval
backbone most commonly paired with Azure OpenAI in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
(RAG) architectures.
Core Building Blocks
Component
Purpose
Data Source
Connection to source data (Blob, ADLS Gen2, SQL, Cosmos DB) for
indexer-based ingestion
Index
Schema defining fields, types, attributes (searchable, filterable,
facetable, retrievable)
Indexer
Scheduled/on-demand pipeline that pulls data, runs skillset, writes to
index
Skillset
Chain of AI enrichment steps (OCR, entity recognition, embeddings,
custom skills)
Knowledge Store
Persists enriched data as JSON/tables in Storage for downstream
analytics
Semantic Ranker
Re-ranks results using deep learning models for improved relevance
Query Capabilities
Full-text search — Lucene query syntax with scoring profiles and analyzers
Vector search — HNSW-based ANN search over embeddings for semantic similarity
Hybrid search — Combines keyword (BM25) + vector search with Reciprocal Rank
Fusion
C L O U D C H E AT S H E E T · A Z U R E
Azure AI Search
Cognitive search & retrieval engine for RAG, enterprise search, and knowledge mining
• 
• 
• 
Mazhar Khan — Cloud Security Portfolio
Page 1 of 3

Semantic ranking — L2 re-ranker improves top-N result ordering using language
understanding
Filters & facets — OData filter expressions on filterable fields for precise narrowing
RAG Architecture Pattern
Typical flow: Document ingestion (Blob) → Indexer + Skillset (chunking, embedding via Azure OpenAI
text-embedding model) → Vector + hybrid index → Query time: user prompt embedded → hybrid search
retrieves top-k chunks → chunks injected into Azure OpenAI chat completion prompt as grounding
context.
Security & Access Control
Private Endpoints — isolate the search service from public internet, integrate with
VNet
Managed Identity — indexer connects to data sources without stored credentials
RBAC roles — Search Service Contributor, Search Index Data Contributor/Reader
(separates admin vs. data-plane access)
Customer-managed keys (CMK) — encrypt index content at rest with Key Vault-held
keys
Document-level security — security trimming via filters mapped to Entra ID group
claims
IP firewall rules — restrict data-plane and control-plane access by CIDR range
Sizing & Tiers Quick Reference
Tier
Notes
Free
3 indexes, shared infra — dev/testing only
Basic
Dedicated, 15 indexes, good for small production workloads
Standard S1–S3
Scales partitions/replicas independently; S3 HD for many small
indexes
Storage Optimized
L1/L2
Large-scale, cost-optimized for high-volume knowledge mining

Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Fix
Chunking too large
for embedding
model context
Chunk 300-800 tokens with overlap; test retrieval precision
empirically
Mixing vector-only
search without
keyword fallback
Use hybrid search; pure vector search underperforms on exact-
match/rare terms
No security
trimming on multi-
tenant index
Apply filters mapped to Entra ID group claims at query time, not just
UI-level restriction
Re-indexing full
corpus on every
change
Use indexer change tracking / incremental high-watermark strategy
Ignoring semantic
ranker for
ambiguous queries
Enable semantic ranking on Standard+ tier for materially better top-
N relevance
Interview / Exam Quick Hits
Replicas scale query throughput and HA; partitions scale storage and indexing
throughput
Vector search uses HNSW algorithm; supports quantization to reduce memory footprint
Skillsets can call custom Web API skills (e.g., Azure Functions) for bespoke
enrichment logic
Semantic ranker is a paid add-on capability, priced per query on Standard tier and
above
Indexers can run incrementally using change tracking columns or Blob soft-delete/
high-watermark
